today_summary: COMEX gold (GCM26) consolidates at $4,354 with ATM IV at 20.92% — the 5-session low — after last week's flush-and-recover cycle. PC OI ratio holds steady at 0.62, confirming persistent call-side dominance and an underlying bullish lean. Max pain anchors at $4,875, a $521 premium over spot that functions as a structural expiry magnet for the remaining 42 DTE. 1σ daily move = $57, bracketing today's session to roughly $4,297–$4,411 absent a macro catalyst.
five_day_thesis: The defining event of this 5-day window was the mid-week flush and recovery: spot collapsed from $4,287 (Jun 10) to $4,116 (Jun 12, −4%) under deeply negative dealer GEX (−291 peak on Jun 11), then fully reclaimed losses by Jun 16–17. That GEX overhang has now almost entirely unwound — aggregate net GEX compressed from −291 to −7.07 — meaning the mechanical amplification that turbocharged the downswing is no longer present. ATM IV was crushed 2.42pp in the aftermath (23.34% → 20.92%), confirming the dip overpriced fear; vol sellers won the week. Max pain drifted lower by $125 (5000 → 4875), a sign that option writers are repricing their neutral zone toward spot, though the $521 gap preserves a meaningful expiry pull. The $4,000 put wall has been absolutely immovable — OI grew 46% to 4,935 contracts across 5 sessions — marking the institutional floor; paired with the static $5,370 call ceiling (8,010 OI), the options architecture telegraphs a wide but clearly bounded range through June expiry.
what_changed: ATM IV −2.42pp to 20.92% (5-session low); post-dip vol crush confirms fear was overpriced at the lows.
Max pain drifted −$125 to $4,875; option writers migrating neutral zone toward spot but $521 gap still intact.
Aggregate net GEX collapsed from −291 to −7.07; dealers near gamma-flat, removing the amplifier that drove the Jun 11–12 selloff.
Spot +$67.5 net for the week (+$238 off the Jun 12 low of $4,116); full recovery with compressed vol into the close.
$4,000 put wall OI grew 46% (3,377 → 4,935); institutional floor buying accelerated into the dip.
Total call OI +18.6% (93,430 → 110,783) vs put OI +15.5% (59,651 → 68,914); call-side growth outpacing, keeping PCR anchored at 0.62.
prediction: Range-bound with mild upward bias into the remainder of June expiry. Local GEX is net-positive between $4,300–$4,395 (call GEX dominant at those strikes), creating a dealer buy-the-dip dynamic and dampening intraday vol. Above $4,400, individual-strike net GEX turns negative (−1.79 at $4,400, −5.45 at $4,450), so a sustained break there would shift dealers to short-gamma hedging and could accelerate to the $4,450–$4,500 zone. Downside is well-defended: nearest put wall is $4,150 (put OI 2,400), major structural floor at $4,000 (4,935 OI). With GEX near-flat globally and IV at cycle lows, the next directional leg requires a macro catalyst — without one, expect the $4,300–$4,420 band to contain price action today.

day_trade_cta.bias_note: Local GEX is positive $4,300–$4,395 (dealers long gamma = buy-the-dip behavior). Above $4,400, GEX flips negative and moves can accelerate. Preferred direction: buy dips toward pivot, target the GEX inflection.
day_trade_cta.entry_zone_note: ATM cluster zone with net_gex +0.36 to +7.27 at $4,350–$4,355. Current spot is consolidating in this band; dip entries within it receive passive dealer support.
day_trade_cta.pivot_note: $4,300 is the dominant call-GEX anchor (net_gex +4.62, call_gex 22.68). A close below $4,300 removes the primary dealer support bid and opens $4,250 (net_gex −7.05, heavy put pressure).
day_trade_cta.stop_note: Clears the $4,280–$4,285 zone where net GEX turns sharply negative (−1.58, −2.35); below $4,270 the tape is in unanchored dealer-short territory with no meaningful put wall until $4,150.
day_trade_cta.targets_note: $4,400 = local GEX inflection (net_gex −1.79; first resistance level within 1σ range). $4,450 = next meaningful call cluster (call_gex 10.85, net_gex −5.45); a break there shifts focus to $4,700 (call OI wall, 4,980 contracts).
